steps.action: Navigate to http://localhost:3100 in headless browser
steps.expected: Page loads with map and UI components
steps.observed: Page loaded but dynamic import stuck on 'INITIALIZING MAP SYSTEM...' loading state due to Next.js dev server chunk 404s (HMR cache invalidation). EVA chrome (header, data readouts) rendered. This is an infrastructure issue, not a code bug.

steps.action: Take screenshot of current loading state
steps.observed: Screenshot saved to zero-places-loading.png showing loading state with NERV header and coordinate readouts visible

Only resource 404 errors for Next.js chunks (main-app.js, app-pages-internals.js, page.js, layout.css) due to HMR cache invalidation. No application JavaScript errors. Font preload warnings (expected in headless Chrome).

steps.action: Review MapView component (map-view.tsx) for zero-places safety
steps.expected: Code handles empty places array without crashes
steps.observed: PASS: visiblePlaces = places.filter(p => activeCategories.has(p.category)) safely returns empty array. allFiltered check only for activeCategories.size === 0. SearchBar receives places prop, CategoryFilterBar is independent of places data.

steps.action: Review PlaceMarkers component (place-markers.tsx) for zero-places safety
steps.expected: Component renders nothing when places array is empty
steps.observed: PASS: placesToRender = visiblePlaces ?? allPlaces; placesToRender.map(...) on empty array returns empty fragment. No unguarded array access (no places[0] or .length checks that could throw).

steps.action: Review SearchBar component (search-bar.tsx) for empty places handling
steps.expected: Search gracefully handles empty places array
steps.observed: PASS: filteredResults filters places array — empty places in produces empty results. When query matches nothing, shows 'NO TARGETS FOUND' message via search-no-results testid. No crashes on empty array.

steps.action: Review CategoryFilterBar component (category-filter-bar.tsx) for zero-places handling
steps.expected: Filter bar renders normally regardless of places count
steps.observed: PASS: CategoryFilterBar iterates over CATEGORY_ORDER constant (6 categories), not over places. Renders all 6 filter buttons regardless of whether any places exist.

steps.expected: Empty state message renders when all categories filtered off
steps.observed: PASS: EmptyStateMessage shows 'NO TARGETS IN RANGE' with 'ENABLE CATEGORY FILTERS TO DISPLAY' subtitle. Rendered when allFiltered (activeCategories.size === 0). With zero places and default categories active, no EmptyStateMessage is shown (correct behavior — the message is for 'all filters off', not 'no data').

steps.action: Verify unit tests cover empty/no-match scenarios
steps.observed: PASS: search-bar.test.tsx has 'shows NO TARGETS FOUND when query matches nothing' test. empty-state-message.test.tsx validates the empty state renders correctly. map-view.test.tsx confirms all components render together.

evidence.consoleErrors: No JavaScript errors. Only Next.js dev server chunk 404s (infrastructure issue, not code bug) and font preload warnings (expected in headless Chrome).

codeReview.mapView: places.filter() on empty array returns []. PlaceMarkers receives empty visiblePlaces. No crashes.
codeReview.placeMarkers: placesToRender.map() on empty array renders nothing. No unguarded array access.
codeReview.searchBar: Empty places array -> any search query -> filteredResults empty -> 'NO TARGETS FOUND' displayed.
codeReview.categoryFilterBar: Independent of places data. Renders all 6 category buttons from CATEGORY_ORDER constant.
codeReview.emptyStateMessage: Shows 'NO TARGETS IN RANGE' when activeCategories.size === 0 (all toggled off). Not shown when places is empty but categories active (expected behavior).

frictions.description: Next.js dev server is serving HTML but returning 404 for all static JS chunks (main-app.js, app-pages-internals.js, page.js) and CSS (layout.css). This is an HMR cache invalidation issue where chunk version hashes in the HTML don't match available files. The dynamic import for MapView never completes, leaving the page stuck on 'INITIALIZING MAP SYSTEM...' loading state.
frictions.resolution: Worked around by performing thorough source code review of all components involved in zero-places scenario. Code analysis confirms the assertion passes. Browser screenshot captured to document the loading state.

blockers.description: Next.js dev server chunk serving broken — all JS/CSS chunks returning 404 (main-app.js, app-pages-internals.js, page.js, layout.css). The dynamic import for MapView component never resolves. Tried: fresh page load, clearing cookies, reload, multiple waits up to 15 seconds. Per subagent guidelines, did not restart the dev server as other subagents may be using it.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Tried: 3 page reloads, cookie clear + fresh navigation, waited 10+ seconds per load, checked network requests. All chunk files consistently 404. Server process (next-server v15.5.12) is running on port 3100 and responds to HTML requests.

summary: Tested 1 assertion (VAL-CROSS-008): PASS via source code review. The browser-based test was blocked by Next.js dev server chunk 404s (infrastructure issue), but comprehensive code analysis of all 5 relevant components (MapView, PlaceMarkers, SearchBar, CategoryFilterBar, EmptyStateMessage) confirms the app handles zero places gracefully: no crashes, no unguarded array access, search shows 'NO TARGETS FOUND', filter bar renders independently, and markers simply don't render. Existing unit tests also validate empty/no-match edge cases.
